markdown.artifact:
# Regulated escrow

A buyer and seller transact through governed escrow; a regulator can view the invoice, two operators witness, and anyone can verify the release without trusting the node.

- **Network:** local (never mainnet)
- **Proof level cap:** L3 (G2)
- **Node trusted:** no — independently verifiable
- **Simulated:** this document describes the flow; it is not a live proof

## Participants

- **Buyer** — user
- **Seller** — user
- **Regulator** — disclosureAuthority
- **Compliance agent** — agent

## Steps

1. **Buyer joins** — actor
2. **Run the governed escrow** — workflow_action (`workflow.execute`)
3. **Buyer approves the release** — approval
4. **Regulator can view the invoice** — disclosure
5. **Two operators witness** — witness
6. **Export the proof receipt** — proof_export
7. **Verify the proof without trusting the node** — proof_verify
8. **Replay the run in Cinema** — cinema_replay
9. **Compliance agent reviews the proof** — inbox_review

## Required approvals

- Buyer — approval (Buyer approves the release)
- Compliance agent — review (Compliance agent reviews the proof)

## Trust boundary

Independently verifiable — the producing node is never trusted. The proof is checked by re-verifying the evidence bytes, not by trusting a server. Local deterministic execution caps at L3: L4 is not reachable without a live L0 anchor.

notes: The producing node is never trusted; proof is verified by re-checking the evidence bytes.
Mainnet is disabled; this flow targets local.
Proof caps at L3: L4 requires a live L0 verify step on Kermit.

version: 1
id: regulated-escrow
title: Regulated escrow
network: local
actors:
    buyer:
        kind: user
    complianceAgent:
        kind: agent
    regulator:
        kind: disclosureAuthority
    seller:
        kind: user
policy:
    releaseRequires:
        - buyerApproval
    disclosure:
        encryptedAtRest: true
        regulatorCanView:
            - invoice
steps:
    - id: run-escrow
      action: workflow.execute
proof:
    requireReplay: true
    requireWitnessThreshold: 2
    minimumLevel: L3
narrative:
    promise: A buyer and seller transact through governed escrow; a regulator can view the invoice, two operators witness, and anyone can verify the release without trusting the node.
    description: Composed in the Visual Workflow Studio (nextux-12).
    whyItMatters: Every step compiles to an existing verified primitive; nothing here bypasses the gated execution paths.
    cinemaLabels:
        run-escrow: Run the governed escrow

notes: The producing node is never trusted; proof is verified by re-checking the evidence bytes.

flow.title: Regulated escrow
flow.description: A buyer and seller transact through governed escrow; a regulator can view the invoice, two operators witness, and anyone can verify the release without trusting the node.

config.goal: run a governed escrow and prove it

nodes.label: Verify the proof without trusting the node

items.expert: actor id + role (user, agent, regulator, witness, operator)
agent action id (e.g. workflow.execute), goal type, inputs
disclosure authority key page + disclosed fields
verifier command, live L0 confirmation (L4) vs local (L3)
proof receipt + verifier command + docs links

requiredApprovals: Compliance agent — review (Compliance agent reviews the proof)
possibleFailures: fewer than the required witnesses attest: Two operators witness
the action does not complete or its policy denies it: workflow.execute
the approver declines or never signs: Buyer approves the release
the proof does not verify (tampered or incomplete evidence): Verify the proof without trusting the node
the replay is not independently reproducible: Replay the run in Cinema
the reviewer rejects or requests changes: Compliance agent reviews the proof
